title: Costing applications
summary: 9706 P2 — unit, job, and batch costing for different production types.
learningObjectives: Distinguish unit, job, and batch costing.
Apply to pricing and quotation decisions.

simpleExplanation.title: Different ways to cost products
simpleExplanation.summary: Unit costing suits identical products; job costing tracks each custom order; batch costing groups similar small runs.

flashcards.back: Mass production of identical items (e.g. chemicals, bricks).
Group similar jobs; cost per batch ÷ units = unit cost.
Unit of output to which cost is assigned (litre, job no., batch).
Record of materials, labour, overheads for one job.

sections.content: **Costing applications** (2.2.1) — choose **method to match production type**. Justify choice in evaluation questions.

sections.question: Batch 47: materials $2,400; direct labour 120 hours @ $15; overheads absorbed at $8 per labour hour. Batch size 600 units. Calculate total cost and cost per unit.
